reviews.review_description: This was honestly a pretty awesome game. The only problem is that you face the same enemies for the whole game which got a little boring especially as you improve. Pretty well done other than that.

reviews.review_title: When calamity gives you enemies, slay.
reviews.review_description: Honestly can't stop playing this, just wish they would add more. I've been wondering what used to be behind that weapons testing dummy in the forge also Agatha says that other forges mustve survived so maybe we can find them?, i also wish we could find survivors to talk to and that once we defeat enough of the beasts we could unlock new maps or other things like maybe a physical shield that you need to have on some maps or a weapons enchanting system

reviews.review_title: Devs broke the game and don't care
reviews.review_description:
This glitch initially happened on the rift/PC version. You can see it if you move your weapon in front of you. It looks like ghosting on the weapon or some kind of botched reprojection. It makes the game really uncomfortable to play.

When I reached out to the devs by email, they confirmed they know about the glitch, and that it was unintentionally introduced with the two-handed weapon update. Their terrible suggestion was to not play the PC version, and play this one instead. I did, and the glitch was initially absent. When I came back to the game after an absence, the glitch is now also VERY present on the Quest version. The devs DO NOT CARE. They keep breaking their own game, and won't fix this issue. I've tried on my Quest 2 and 3.

FIX YOUR GAME.

developer_response.body: Sorry to hear you are experiencing a visual issue within the game. It sounds like something might not be working quite right. We'd love to learn more about what could be going on so we can help troubleshoot the problem. Please reach out to our player support team at https://bit.ly/4qEHxbO to work through the issue.

reviews.review_description: Nice to get a good workout while enjoying it.

Until You Fall is easily one of the best VR games I’ve played. The controls are fairly easy to learn, the combat mechanics are awesome, and the environment is well-made. If you’re new to VR or really want a close-combat sword fighting game, this is for you.

There’s not a lot of great games for people stuck on the Comfortable level, as in, you have to play games that doesn’t trigger your motion sickness. This is one of those great games. On the parts/dashes that moves you from one enemy to another, you can close you eyes for a brief second to avoid motion sickness. Open your eyes, and get back into action. You can adjust the vignette pretty significantly (is that how it’s spelled) to minimize motion sickness as well. 

Due to its comfort level, this game is indeed one of the best VR games for beginners and players who want quick gameplay that gives you a workout and a taste of some adrenaline-inducing action. It’s rogue-like, meaning when you die, you start over again. Each time, you’re able to choose upgrades as you go. I quickly realized I hated this because I lose some stuff, but it is really satisfying when you have good upgrades going. Although I said quick gameplay, some people can spend hours in this. So, it’s really nice to be able to spend as little or as much time here as you want.

This game is no longer supported anymore (maybe just small patches to make sure it actually opens and works after Quest updates, but no more quality updates for this game). So if you encounter glitches… it’s probably not likely to get addressed. Just uninstall and reinstall again. Sucks, but if it’s on sale, I’d get it. 

Played this when I had the Quest+ subscription last year, and had been thinking about how good it was ever since. Bought it with my own money recently, and still wasn’t disappointed. I never tried to grow out of the Comfortable level because I don’t spend a lot of time on the Quest. I’m just a 20-60 min on my day off here and there kind of person.

reviews.review_title: Fun but repetitive— PLEASE UPDATE IT!
reviews.review_description:
I came here to leave a review in hopes that I can reach the devs.

This game is so fun. It burns a few calories. But it’s got the same ~9 levels and ~10 enemies that you fight over and over again. I’d absolutely buy a sequel or a DLC. 

I’m not hopeful since the game feels somewhat abandoned: it used to have a dedicated discord server but now that’s been archived and folded into the bigger 
Schell games one. Only one social media post about it in the last year. Etc

This game falls into the category of must haves, like Beat Saber, Synth Rides or Pistol Whip. It is not music based but combat based, it is similar in terms of how easy it is to boot and get "in the loop". Will provide you with a good workout , too :) At the same time it has depth capacity (different enemies, difficulties and most important - lots of weapons and build combinations around them. 

Critical notice: QGO (Quest Games Optimizer) is a must here. Without it graphics on Quest 3 is okay-ish, but with QGO it looks way better.

However, some drawbacks:
- "rooms" are the same each run. Not that critical for what the game is, but it would be nice to have more procedurally generated objects/rooms with greater diversity.
- would be nice to have more enemy archetypes.
- sadly, but developers do not support this game, and do not work on the next parts or something like this in terms of gameplay. It is polished and finished as a product, but you will not get more in any form.

AAAAND to feel cool af lol it really is my new go to for exercise! I strap on my 2.5 lbs wrist weights and tighten the abs and go to TOOOOOWN!!! 27 hours of game time and I've ALMOST beaten the boss on easy 😆 played it for 4 hours on the meta+ subscription then it was removed and those 4 hours made it worth me buying the actual game and not rent it from meta till they decide to RIP it away from me lol
As soon as I played the free trial I KNEW I had to buy it and a few months in, not a single regret. Two words: MUST. BUY!!!!

I played this a long time ago and loved it, recently I got the Meta Quest 3 and found this game to be one of my favourites still.

I'm pretty sure this game hasn't got any Quest 3 updates but I hope they come out with some soon or that a Sequel game comes out.
One of my most played vr games. PRESS THE ADVANTAGE!

Released in 2020 !!! And YET nothing comparable.
Juicy game !! I'm just to poor to success a completed run ;(

reviews.review_title: One of my favourite games in VR EVER!!!!
reviews.review_description: As a man with cerebral palsy I spend about 95% of the game fighting with my right arm so I absolutely love that the blocking is designed the way it is because I’m fast enough to block most attacks with my one arm, the progression with the aether is also well done as it feels like you can grind your way into progressing through the levels by leveling up your weapons. I can’t wait to see what the future holds for this game!!!!!!

I’m a VR newbie and have motion sickness but this game is sooooo cool and it definitely can make you sweat. The upgrade system is a little confusing you can only upgrade whichever weapon you’re holding on your left hand if I understand it correctly. Cost me a bit to learn it but overall it’s awesome. Great experience.

In this visually stunning VR game you fight a neon world. The fighter system is comfortable but challenging to master, so progress provides a rewarding experience. This is incredibly addictive, whether you are playing for 10 minutes or a couple hours. Surely a 5-star game!

reviews.review_title: Game was 5 stars all day long, now 1!
reviews.review_description:
This game was one of the best out but that all changed when it started glitching. When I get to the very end boss and it's heart jumps out of his chest for the final death blow he recovers every time because no matter how I swing on it or I hit it or anything I tried every angle it's like I'm not even touching it and so he recovers over and over and over and I'm unable to kill him so due to this glitch, until it's fixed, I'll be at one star.

P.S. this is happening on all difficulty settings!

reviews.review_title: It's good but I've got a problem
reviews.review_description: I got the free trial a while ago and loved it so I saved up to buy it and I bought it but now every time I launch it it just says trail over but I bought the game with my money I'm sure of it my acc got charged and everything

developer_response.body: Sorry to hear you're running into this issue! Please drop our Player Support team a line at https://bit.ly/4eLlQBP so we can work through the problem with you.

reviews.review_description: Movement and gameplay is fun and easy to get used to.

reviews.review_title: The Reason Why This Helps You Burn Fat
reviews.review_description:
So Some Say This game helps then lose fat and stuff but they never talk about how this game is helping then improve their fighting and stuff but yeah buy this game if you want to improve your fighting and losing fat
:)

Any game that I can play for hours and makes me want to get back in VR is a 5 stars for me. Could it be better? Sure. Could it be easier to learn? Sure. But once you get the hang of it, it offers a really unforgiving and rewarding experience.

reviews.review_title: Great game but I have suggestion
reviews.review_description: Is it too hard to develop a sandbox when you can play with selected enemies and choosing their numbers…?
SO MUCH FUN

reviews.review_title: Pick up your weapon, we're going again..
reviews.review_description: Until You Fall is a visually stunning VR game that’s as addictive as it is immersive. With its neon-soaked, geometric world and fast-paced sword fighting. The intuitive combat system is easy to pick up, but challenging to master. I would recommend to anyone who wants 10 minutes from the couch or an addictive dive into hours of combat.
Only gripe I have is this: there is no real "until you fall" mode. Something like infinite waves of enemies.

reviews.review_title: What about new things for an update.
reviews.review_description:
Im already beat the nightmare mode, but im gonna make an good idea to make this game even more for everyone.

I just wish they gonna make a big update like newer enemies, maps, traps, bosses, new character for quest, main quest, and daily quest.

And add the endless mode that can become endless fun for our workout.

Super addicting once you get into it.

At first, I didn’t like the concept of having to start over when i fall lol. But over each run you collect more aether and you can upgrade your weapons and get better. I kept doing that until I was able to beat the final boss and it’s so satisfying. And even after completing the game, I still want to upgrade my other weapons and try different combos. It is a workout, especially if you play like I do. I have really buff shoulders now from doing large swings and I squat and engage my core whenever I can. I can burn like 400 cal in an hour. This is a must get game!

I hope they add more!

I enjoyed the game until I realized that if one of the main villains kills you, you have to start all over again from the previous 4-5 levels. This is not only very frustrating and something I don't feel like doing, but it's also a huge disappointment because I loved this game. I don't understand the developers' mindset; why do you have to fight through the levels you've already completed? It completely took away my enjoyment of the whole game....
I found the dialogue painfully boring and the combat was very repetetive and strenuous.

The game has lots of polish, and it's extremely fun. Most of the weapons are viable and super fun to use, the overall game is great and it gets tough on higher difficulties. Overall a must play in my opinion.

I wish the devs still supported it, so we could get a Q3 optimization and visuals update (maybe even extra content).

reviews.review_title: Fake reviews , no way this is 4.7 stars
reviews.review_description:
This "game" is nothing more than a repetitious, basic repeatedcmovemtscagainst the worlds stupidest ai.
It is clearly a budget title, without a budget price tag.
It is just an ongoing "room" after "room" of the same 3 or 4 standing enemies swinging in their pre determined  paths, matching a swing pattern in an unrealistic sword path.
More like a Simon says setup than a free range combat mechanism, it is the dullest experience I've had on vr.
The graphics and animation is terrible, hitting an enemy makes it move slightly backward but no real visual of any contact or damage at all. Repeat x 1 million. Die then go through a walk through "lobby thing" wait for the repeated txt and do some more of the same.
I wouldn't call it a game, more of a "hit same object simulmator".
Glad it was in meta+, as I can delete and move on. Don't pay your hard earned cash or fall for the numerous fake high reviews. This is rubbish.
There’s way too many bugs,you can hit where your suppose to hit with a 3 handed weapon but it simply does not register it.  Way too many restraints and bugs to have any fun.

reviews.review_title: Games look abandoned. No Quest 3 updates
reviews.review_description:
Love the game play but games look abandoned. 
No Quest 3 updates
No new content for users to come back to
No updates at all in 2 years

The game is great, but it lacks updates and new content. It gets repetitive quickly, as all the levels look the same, and the characters feel very similar too. Some bHaptics support would be appreciated at least.
fun!

Used to love this game.  Picked it up again after a few weeks break, and it's unplayable.  Weapon disappears at random, doesn't block even when in position, and enemy attacks don't even show a prompt.  Had elite mace enemy instantly use its AoE twice for unavoidable damage.  Weapon vanishing can cause an instant loss against multi-hit combos because you get zero i-frames after being hit (an inexcusable design problem imo).

The game is artificially difficult and makes the player feel like they are being cheated out a deserved win.  Older good reviews are no longer valid.  Avoid at all costs until the developer does a major overhaul.

developer_response.body: Hello there and thank you for your patience in getting back to you! We'd really appreciate it if you could send in a report to us by emailing gamesupport@schellgames.com so we can assist.

reviews.review_title: I was surprised how repetitive it is
reviews.review_description: You always start from the same levels, and clear them untill you fall. Rinse and repeat, 20-30 min of the same run every time, same monsters, same layout, same composition.

There's like 7 weapons, no customization, no shield, very repetitive. They could make the game much better by adding these things, I wish games would let you a play through trial because I would have never bought this.

I bought this game because there was so many good reviews for it. Don’t let that fool you, it kinda sucks.the movement is bad, so is the combat and weapon mechanics, I wish I had not bought it and I never play it,

tl;dr i found the game anxiety-inducing even on relaxed mode, doesn't teach any basic fighting skills despite ending sparring/hacking, and very confusing on how to progress.


i wanted a fun exercise/sword game where i can hack things but also learn some basic sword fighting.

this game is fun, it does give you a lot of exercise, but the reason for two stars are:

(1) it really triggers my anxiety even on "relaxed" mode, when multiple boss monsters crowd you it gets really scary and leaves you feeling trapped and hopeless.  it would be great to have a SUPER relaxed mode where you always fight one on one.

(2) the sword play in the app is basically non-existent, you match the orientation of your weapons with infrequent suggestions on the screen, you won't get any actual basic training in fighting or countering someone with weapons.  why can't there just be a sword game against an AI that's actually good at sword fighting?

(3) it is really arbitrary when a monster takes damage.  i do the same thing on fodder monsters -- dash to them, left chop right chop; sometimes the monster dies right away, sometimes it doesn't for a long long time. none of the actual gameplay is very well explained.  when is soul burn activated?  why do monsters regain full health after i just hacked their last bits of health away?

reviews.review_title: Weird and disappointing tiny hand bug.
reviews.review_description:
The game seems interesting but I have a very odd bug that won't go away; everything is tiny. My hands are tiny. The enemies are tiny. My swords swing around half a feet away from my tiny hands. The controllers change from a normal size to the size of my thumbs right after choosing the sitting or standing mode, making the game completely unplayable. 
How do I get a refund?

Game have nice graphic and fight system but its ONLY 1 (ONE) type of game. You go forward and kill few monsters and go to the next room. It have near 20 levels with little different look. But when you play 10 times it's getting boring. Devs why you writing that you trying to make perfect fight game, solo player game etc etc while game it's nice but for few hours only? That game need other game types , other levels, maybe coop . Now it's not worth that money. I never played swords of Gargantua but they have coop. It's more cool when you can play with other guys.
really very unattractive

The fighting system absolutely sucks. Instead of a realistic blocking system where you respond to your opponents movements they have these blocking signals that appear and you have to put your weapons in that exact spot and shape to block. You could have two longswords both raised up above your head and the opponent will slice right through both your weapons like they aren't there at all if they aren't directly on the blue lines. Really dumb. 

Also bought this because it was supposed to be a good workout. I played for an hour straight and didn't even break a sweat. It's very slow and boring.
The game rec room has better sword fighting game play
The game tells you which direction it wants you to swipe your controller. It doesn't feel like melee combat; it feels like a cheap mobile game.

I played this game for two minutes and was so sick for 24 hours after, I couldn't do anything but lay in bed. It's pretty with visuals and the game tries to warn you about motion sickness with a vignetting option but it made no difference to me whatsoever. I wish I could recommend but I will never try this game again. Back to beat saber for my VR exercise.

I didn't understand how to change difficulty level (challenging by default). Many options available but none for difficulty.
Just die and die during first minutes.
Enemies movements not predictible.
I try to block with both hands but nothing block, i just die.
Have to keep button press always or weapon disappear.
(on oculus quest 2)
All my experience was bad in this game.

[edit]
After some tries, i think i understand :
- You cannot block with any sword (not sure)
- What you see and what you think good to do is not important, only indications of the game mater to block attack, if you respect it, it works, if you follow your feelings, it fail.
So the game is not a sword game but more a follow instructions as fast as possible game.

[edit]
You can change difficulty only after first level.

reviews.review_title: Was good at first but impossible
reviews.review_description: Ok imma start by saying its a great game and all and it really is good but I’ve been playing for the last 49 hours and still haven’t completed it ON EASY. Ive been searching for the past hour to see who has completed this and i haven’t found a single video yet. Summary is that the game is good but after one day it’s ridiculously hard and near impossible to complete. Would not recommend

developer_response.body:
Thank you for your feedback, XxyounisxX.
The Unraveled can be relentless and nightmarish, but you can defeat them! Stay strong, Champion! I know you can do this.

I would love to point you towards our Discord server and the #battle-tactics channel within it. Here, many other Rune Knights work together to share helpful battle tips, strategies, and weapon composition recommendations for both new and veteran players alike. In fact, many of the active Rune Knights here have slain countless Aether Horrors and are eager to assist and encourage others on doing the same.
We'd love to see you there: https://discord.gg/untilyoufall

reviews.review_description:
So I wasn't really sure what this game was going to be, but I'd say to make sure your expectations are in order it's closer to beat sabre in function than say Zelda or dark souls. You will fight in the same arenas over and over with different mixtures of enemies. Now for some you may say, that sounds dull. But honestly it's less about the location and more about the challenge. Much like beat sabre where you are always in the same place, interacting with the same things, but it's always a bit different.
The combat is fun, the weapons feel good and there is something unbelievably satisfying about grabbing upgrade crystals. Visually they have done a lot with very little. It's very smart design and all the different enemies are unique enough that once you start coming across mixed groups you really start to appreciate the different approaches needed for all of them.
Movement is very comfortable, I sometimes feel vr sickness but in this I haven't experienced it. It's not too much of a work out, more than superhot, not as much as beat sabre. 
The soundtrack is very cool and had me bopping along multiple times. It's the sort of game that I know I'll just keep coming back to long after getting to the end.

The save system is terrible and only like that because they need it to seem like a longer game.  The weapons are not very fun your just going to be using the first 2 the whole time.  Graphics are not that good and you’ll be raging every step of the way.  Don’t waste your money.

Movement limited to the Path. The blows Under the belt are not contemplated and the fighting system is boring.
25 euro thrown in the loo
I didn't like it

New to vr. This game is a lot of fun and a good challenge. Makes you feel very powerful. Will be playing for many hours! I really like that the game makes you try a lot of different fighting styles through the different combos of wepons. Great game!
I have been playing this all day. It’s so well done and makes you feel powerful! It’s challenging, fun, and interactive.
I feel like I’m in an isekai every time I play this game 😆😆 love it !!!
I haven’t made it through the whole game but so far it’s really good

It flows pretty nicely and I really enjoy the way the combat works with the dashes and blocking, makes you think about your attacks instead of just hacking and slashing. All in all a lot of fun and keeps you fit.

reviews.review_title: Most fun single player game since Zelda
reviews.review_description: I think Zelda BoTW is the best game I have played, but this is the best VR game I have played to date. I'm new to VR. I beat a few games but have not played a lot of titles yet. This game is so much fun. I feel really powerful like in Shadow of Mordor but playing in VR is another level. I get so into the game, with blocking, stabbing, and dodging out of the way, that you'll end up getting a little cardio in as a bonus. (or a negative depending on your point of view) I would make sure you have an abundance of room when playing this game. I find myself taking big swings that quickly spin me around for more powerful attacks and fast combos. I would avoid walking into a room where someone is playing this title.
Get it ,and experience it for yourself! No regrets here シ ✰✰✰✰✰
